perturbation.original_text: mPhase agrees to maintain the quality of its Goods associated with the Lucent Co-Branding Logo to a level of quality comparable to the current quality of their Goods.
perturbation.changed_text: mPhase shall endeavor to maintain the quality of its Products associated with the Lucent Co-Branding Logo, but is not required to ensure that the Products are equivalent in quality to the company's current Goods.
perturbation.explanation: The substitution of 'Goods' with 'Products', and replacing the requirement for 'maintaining quality' to equivalent levels with 'endeavor to maintain' creates an in-text contradiction. 'Goods' and 'Products' are used interchangeably, it weakens the obligation on mPhase and introduces uncertainty regarding the required quality standards. Section 7 originally places a clear mandate for equivalent quality, while the perturbation introduces leniency. This directly affects Lucent's rights and the enforceability of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If Lucent determines that mPhase's Goods are no longer maintained at the current level of quality, Lucent shall so notify mPhase, in writing, and Lucent shall have the right to terminate this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates maintaining the quality of 'Goods' associated with the Lucent Co-Branding Logo to a level comparable to the current quality. The changed text weakens this obligation by using 'Products' interchangeably with 'Goods', stating mPhase shall 'endeavor to maintain' the quality, and removing the requirement for equivalent quality. This contradicts the contradicted text, which gives Lucent the right to terminate the agreement if mPhase's 'Goods' are no longer maintained at the current level of quality. The change introduces ambiguity and reduces mPhase's responsibility, potentially voiding Lucent's right to terminate the agreement if the quality is only slightly diminished, and not necessarily breaching the now less stringent 'endeavor to maintain' clause.

perturbation.original_text: mPhase shall have the right to annually renew this agreement for a period of one year upon each annual expiration with the written consent of Lucent, which written consent shall not be unreasonably withheld.
perturbation.changed_text: mPhase may request to annually extend this agreement for a period of one year upon each annual expiration at Lucent's sole and absolute discretion. Lucent may arbitrarily deny the request to extend, for any reason or for no reason at all, with no recourse available to mPhase.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'shall have the right to annually renew' with 'may request to annually extend' and 'written consent shall not be unreasonably withheld' with 'Lucent's sole and absolute discretion' introduces a contradiction. The original text gives mPhase a right to renewal, while the modification makes renewal entirely discretionary for Lucent, contradicting the balance of power initially established in the original text. This creates uncertainty about the agreement's longevity and enforceability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Term of this Agreement will commence on the date above, and shall continue for a term of one (1) year. mPhase shall have the right to annually renew this agreement for a period of one year upon each annual expiration with the written consent of Lucent, which written consent shall not be unreasonably withheld. Either party wishing to terminate the Agreement must give written notice to the other party at least thirty (30) days prior to the desired date of termination.
perturbation.justification: The original agreement grants mPhase a right to renew the agreement with Lucent's consent, which cannot be unreasonably withheld. The modified agreement replaces this right with a request that Lucent may deny at its sole discretion for any reason. This directly contradicts the original renewal terms in the Contradicted Text, which state that 'mPhase shall have the right to annually renew this agreement...which written consent shall not be unreasonably withheld.' The change creates uncertainty about mPhase's ability to renew the agreement, making the enforceability of the renewal clause questionable, thus a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Upon termination of this Agreement, mPhase shall immediately cease use of the Lucent Co-Branding Logo, provided, however, that mPhase shall have no obligation to remove such Co-Branding Logo from any Goods sold prior to the date of such termination.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon expiration of this contract, mPhase is immediately authorized to continue use of the Lucent Co-Branding Logo, unless explicitly notified to cease usage in writing, however, that mPhase shall have no obligation to remove such Co-Branding Logo from any Goods sold prior to the date of such termination.
perturbation.explanation: The substitution of 'termination' with 'expiration' and 'shall immediately cease use' with 'is immediately authorized to continue use, unless explicitly notified to cease usage in writing' creates an in-text contradiction. The modification is intended to flip the assumption when ending the contract, going from cease of use to continue use and requires writing.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon termination of this Agreement, mPhase shall immediately cease use of the Lucent Co-Branding Logo, provided, however, that mPhase shall have no obligation to remove such Co-Branding Logo from any Goods sold prior to the date of such termination. -2-
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text and contradicted text state that upon termination of the agreement, mPhase shall immediately cease use of the Lucent Co-Branding Logo. However, the changed text states that upon expiration of the contract, mPhase is immediately authorized to continue use of the Lucent Co-Branding Logo, unless explicitly notified to cease usage in writing. This creates a direct conflict regarding mPhase's right to use the logo after the contract ends, fitting the Misaligned Terminology definition, as the terms 'termination' and 'expiration' are used to create contradictory obligations.
